claim: Off-the-shelf embedding models cluster signals by structural similarity rather than meaning, causing error tracking data to cluster separately from Slack messages and session replays.
actionable: When grouping diverse signal types in agentic pipelines, generate LLM-based queries from signals before embedding them rather than embedding the signals directly to achieve semantic clustering across modalities.
source_title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog

claim: Error tracking is immediately actionable; Slack and session replay are usually not actionable without additional context and specificity because they describe generic problems rather than specific failures.
actionable: Assess actionability of signals before passing them to code-generation agents by checking whether they point to specific, reproducible failures or describe vague user experiences.
source_title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog

claim: Agent-based experimentation is expensive upfront but reveals patterns that allow collapsing expensive agent-based steps into one-shot LLM calls or trained models, reducing cost significantly.
actionable: Run agents on the same problem 100 times to identify patterns, then collapse expensive steps into cheaper one-shot calls or fine-tuned models once patterns stabilize.
source_title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog

claim: Using Claude Agent SDK in a sandbox with MCP servers to pull in extra data (logs, session replays, codebase context) makes research agent results significantly more accurate.
actionable: Equip agentic systems researching code issues with MCP servers that can access logs, session data, and codebase context to ground analysis in actual facts rather than inference.
source_title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog

claim: Iterating on agent-based pipelines requires evals on representative production data; local vibe-checking produces feedback that does not reflect real-world behavior across diverse customer data.
actionable: Set up evaluation harnesses that test agent pipelines on representative customer data and production signals rather than relying on subjective local testing.
source_title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
